name: ARK Flow — Optical Flow + ToF Distance Sensor
description: Made in the USA, NDAA compliant DroneCAN optical flow and short-range distance sensor. Combines PAW3902 optical flow sensor, Broadcom AFBR-S50LV85D ToF LiDAR, and BMI088 IMU. CAN protocol. PX4 and ArduPilot support. Firmware updates over DroneCAN. Gold standard for NDAA-compliant GPS-denied navigation.
ndaa_note: Designed and manufactured USA. Full supply chain documentation available.

name: ARK Flow MR — Mid-Range Optical Flow + Distance Sensor
description: Drop-in upgrade to ARK Flow with extended 50m mid-range distance sensing. PAA3905 optical flow sensor (improved over PAW3902), Broadcom AFBR-S50LX85D 50m ToF sensor, InvenSense IIM-42653 IMU. Adds only 5g over base ARK Flow. Launched March 2025. Best NDAA-compliant option for outdoor and semi-outdoor GPS-denied operations.
ndaa_note: Designed and manufactured USA. March 2025 release.

name: CubePilot HereFlow — Optical Flow + LiDAR
description: Finger-sized 1.2g optical flow sensor with integrated LiDAR and 6D IMU. PMW3901 optical flow (80mm to infinity), short-range LiDAR altimetry, ICM20602 IMU. CAN protocol. NDAA 2024 compliant — all chips and components sourced and assembled in allied nations (Taiwan). Best lightweight option for small/micro platforms. Note: built-in LiDAR is very short range outdoors; pair with external longer-range LiDAR for outdoor use.
ndaa_note: Manufactured Taiwan. 2024 NDAA compliant — allied nations supply chain.

name: Holybro H-Flow — Optical Flow + Distance Sensor
description: Compact DroneCAN optical flow and distance sensor. PixArt PAA3905E1 optical flow, Broadcom AFBR-S50LV85D ToF distance sensor, InvenSense ICM-42688-P 6-axis IMU. Onboard IR LED for low-light. Compatible with PX4 and ArduPilot. Good component selection (same sensors as ARK Flow MR) but Holybro HQ is Shenzhen, China — supply chain not independently verified for NDAA compliance. Verify before federal procurement.
ndaa_note: Holybro HQ: Shenzhen, China. Supply chain not independently verified. Do not use for federal contracts without written NDAA compliance documentation from Holybro.

name: Centeye Vision Chip Suite — Neuromorphic Optical Flow
description: Washington DC-based pioneer — first company to fly an optical flow sensor on a drone (1990s). Produces proprietary neuromorphic vision chips designed ground-up for drone autonomy. Sensors from 1g. Up to 150° FOV. IR illumination for dark environments (850nm, up to 5m range). Stereo depth + optical flow combined. Not a commodity product — sold as integrated solution or OEM chipset for custom platforms. Contact for pricing. Best option for nano/micro platforms and DARPA-adjacent programs.
ndaa_note: Designed and built USA. OEM/custom integration only.

name: Matek 3901-L0X — Optical Flow + LiDAR (UART)
description: PMW3901 optical flow + VL53L0X LiDAR combined module. UART interface, MSP V2 protocol. iNAV and ArduPilot compatible. Very popular in hobbyist/FPV community for indoor position hold. NOT NDAA compliant — designed and manufactured in China (Mateksys, Shenzhen). Do not use for federal contracts or federally-funded programs. Listed for awareness — this is what most people use, but it cannot be used in NDAA contexts.
ndaa_note: China-made. Not NDAA compliant. Hobbyist use only.

name: DJI O3 / Downward Vision (Integrated) — DJI Platforms Only
description: DJI's integrated downward visual positioning system on Mavic/Mini/Inspire/Matrice series. Combines downward-facing stereo cameras with ultrasonic sensors for indoor hover and obstacle avoidance. Not a standalone purchasable sensor — integrated into DJI platforms only. Not NDAA compliant. Listed for reference as the consumer benchmark most users know.
ndaa_note: China-made, FCC Covered List. Not NDAA compliant. Not available as standalone component.

name: Holybro PMW3901 — UART Optical Flow (Budget)
description: Simple UART PMW3901 breakout with JST-GH connector for Pixhawk TELEM ports. No integrated rangefinder — requires external distance sensor. Budget option for non-NDAA builds.
ndaa_note: Holybro is Chinese-owned. Not NDAA compliant.

name: Bitcraze Flow Deck V2 — PMW3901 + VL53L1X
description: Bitcraze's flow module for Crazyflie nano-drones. Swedish manufacturer. PMW3901 + VL53L1X. Not compatible with PX4/ArduPilot — Crazyflie-only form factor. Good for research/swarm prototyping.
ndaa_note: Bitcraze is Swedish. Generally NDAA compliant but verify PixArt chip sourcing for specific program requirements.

name: MicoAir MTF-01 — Optical Flow + 3m LiDAR
description: Popular budget Chinese flow sensor. Multi-protocol UART (MAVLink/MSP/Micolink). PMW3901 + 3m ToF LiDAR. Excellent value for non-NDAA builds. Available on AliExpress/Amazon ~$22.
ndaa_note: MicoAir is Chinese. Not NDAA compliant.

name: MicoAir MTF-02P — Optical Flow + 6m LiDAR
description: Upgraded MTF-01 with 6m laser rangefinder vs 3m. 42° FOV, min 60 lux. Max 7 m/s at 1m altitude. Multi-protocol UART. Best-value GPS-denied sensor for non-compliant builds.
ndaa_note: MicoAir is Chinese. Not NDAA compliant.

name: ThoneFlow-3901U — PMW3901 UART Breakout
description: Basic UART PMW3901 breakout. No rangefinder. PX4-verified via SENS_TFLOW_CFG parameter. Cheap but requires external distance sensor for altitude. Chinese-made.

name: AlienTek ATK-PMW3901 — PMW3901 SPI/I2C
description: Chinese electronics maker. PMW3901 SPI + integrated (low-quality) distance sensor. PX4 docs recommend external rangefinder. Budget option for DIY/non-compliant builds.

name: CUAV PX4FLOW 2.1 — Camera-Based Optical Flow
description: Camera-based optical flow (not ASIC-based). Uses MT9V034 camera with onboard STM32 processor running Lucas-Kanade optical flow algorithm. Higher resolution than PMW3901 ASIC but heavier/larger. CUAV is Chinese.
ndaa_note: CUAV is Chinese. Not NDAA compliant.

name: Pimoroni PMW3901 Breakout — SPI
description: UK-made dev/maker board. Not a UAV flight controller module — requires custom code (Python library provided). Good for prototyping on Raspberry Pi companion computers. No rangefinder.

name: ARK Pi6X Flow — FC + Integrated Optical Flow Bundle
description: All-in-one NDAA-compliant flight controller stack: ARKV6X FC + Pi CM4 carrier + ARK Flow sensors integrated on one board. Eliminates separate flow module. USA-made.
ndaa_note: Fully NDAA compliant. Made in USA.
